name: OptionsProvider
summary: An OptionsProvider class provides various kinds of options on a Model. You get an OptionsProvider from the OptionsManager. The options are given as name/value pairs. List of keys added in different SketchUp versions: UnitsOptions AreaUnit (SketchUp 2019.2) VolumeUnit (SketchUp 2019.2) AreaPrecision (SketchUp 2020.0) VolumePrecision (SketchUp 2020.0) The AreaUnit and VolumeUnit options in UnitsOptions only applies if the UnitFormat is Length::Decimal.

methods.name: add_observer
methods.summary: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.
methods.remarks: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: count
methods.summary: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module.
methods.remarks: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module. Prior to that the #count method is an alias for #length.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: The #each method is used to iterate through all of the options.
methods.remarks: The #each method is used to iterate through all of the options.

methods.name: each_key
methods.summary: The #each_key method is used to iterate through all of the attribute keys.
methods.remarks: The #each_key method is used to iterate through all of the attribute keys.

methods.name: each_pair
methods.summary: The #each method is used to iterate through all of the options.
methods.remarks: The #each method is used to iterate through all of the options.

methods.name: each_value
methods.summary: The #each_value method is used to iterate through all of the attribute values.
methods.remarks: The #each_value method is used to iterate through all of the attribute values.

methods.name: has_key?
methods.summary: The #has_key? method is an alias for #key?.
methods.remarks: The #has_key? method is an alias for #key?.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of the key you are looking for.

methods.name: key?
methods.summary: The #key? method is used to determine if the options provider has a specific key.
methods.remarks: The #key? method is used to determine if the options provider has a specific key.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of the key you are looking for.

methods.name: keys
methods.summary: The #keys method is used to retrieve an array with all of the attribute keys.
methods.remarks: The #keys method is used to retrieve an array with all of the attribute keys.

returns.doc: keys - an array of keys within the options provider if successful

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method is an alias of #size.
methods.remarks: The #length method is an alias of #size.

methods.name: name
methods.summary: The name method is used to retrieve the name of an options provider.
methods.remarks: The name method is used to retrieve the name of an options provider.

returns.doc: name - the name of the options provider if successful

methods.name: remove_observer
methods.summary: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.
methods.remarks: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: size
methods.summary: The #size method is used to retrieve the size (number of elements) of an options provider.
methods.remarks: The #size method is used to retrieve the size (number of elements) of an options provider.

properties.summary: The [] method is used to get a value by name or index of the key.
properties.remarks: The [] method is used to get a value by name or index of the key.
